rationale: This is the prototypical Pinacol rearrangement, using pinacol (2,3-dimethylbutane-2,3-diol) itself as the substrate. Under acidic conditions (e.g., sulfuric acid), one hydroxyl group is protonated and leaves as water, forming a tertiary carbocation. A methyl group then undergoes a [1,2]-shift to the adjacent carbocation center, and deprotonation of the remaining hydroxyl group yields the product, pinacolone (3,3-dimethyl-2-butanone). This example is the simplest and most direct illustration of the reaction, defining the entire class.
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses the simplest possible acyclic, aliphatic substrate (pinacol) to avoid overlap with other named reactions. It avoids rings (preventing confusion with Tiffeneau-Demjanov or other ring expansions), arenes (avoiding Wagner-Meerwein rearrangements in complex systems), and other functional groups. The simplicity of the tetramethyl-substituted diol is highly specific to the Pinacol rearrangement.
